title: cicuta virosa var. angustifolia
intro: Cicuta virosa var. angustifolia, also known as narrow-leaved water hemlock, is a highly toxic and invasive plant species native to North America. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate the nervous system and act as an antispasmodic. However, due to its high toxicity, it is essential to use this plant under the guidance of an experienced herbalist or medical professional, as it can cause serious health issues and even death if ingested in large quantities. Herbal preparations of cicuta virosa var. angustifolia are typically made into tinctures or infusions, but these should only be used in small amounts and with caution.
